upgrades.edge_index_hint: William E. Ladd -> Boston Children's Hospital Department of Surgery (institutional_founder, 1927-1945)

match_fields.target_node: Boston Children's Hospital Department of Surgery

upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 3095882 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Prog Pediatr Surg 1986 | content_match] Bill H. 'William E. Ladd, M.D.: great pioneer of North American pediatric surgery.' Documents Ladd's appointment as Surgeon-in-Chief at Boston Children's Hospital and establishment of the first pediatric surgical training program.

upgrades.edge_index_hint: Harold Gillies -> Queen's Hospital Facial Reconstruction Unit (institutional_founder, 1917-1925)
upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 25858011 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | J Laryngol Otol 2015 | content_match] 'Sir Harold Delf Gillies, the otolaryngologist and father of modern facial plastic surgery: review of his rhinoplasty case notes.' Documents Gillies establishing the facial injury ward at Queen's Hospital, Sidcup, founding modern plastic surgery.

upgrades.edge_index_hint: Robert E. Gross -> W. Hardy Hendren III (direct_training, 1955-1960)
upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 3095885 on 2026-03-18] [VERIFY_NAMES: confirm Hendren named as Gross trainee] Clatworthy HW. 'Robert E. Gross.' Prog Pediatr Surg. 1986;20:76-84. Documents Gross's career at Boston Children's and his training lineage. Hendren succeeded Gross as Chief of Surgery at Boston Children's (1982), establishing the mentor-trainee relationship.

upgrades.edge_index_hint: Robert E. Gross -> C. Everett Koop (direct_training, 1946-1947)
upgrades.notes_append: [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 3095885 on 2026-03-18] [VERIFY_NAMES: confirm Koop named as Gross trainee] Clatworthy HW. 'Robert E. Gross.' Prog Pediatr Surg. 1986;20:76-84. Koop's brief fellowship under Gross at Boston Children's (1946-1947) is documented in biographical sources. Previously downgraded in Phase 2.
